text: Half-marathon performance in male recreational runners can be predicted with 96.2% accuracy using a combination of training, anthropometric, physiological, and biomechanical variables.

Physiological variables (VO2max, speed at anaerobic threshold, peak speed) predict half-marathon performance better than training/anthropometric or biomechanical variables alone, explaining 94.9% of variance.
Biomechanical variables (contact time, flight time, step length, step rate) explain 93.7% of variance in half-marathon performance, nearly as much as physiological variables.
Training volume and anthropometric variables alone can predict half-marathon performance with 90.3% accuracy, making them useful when laboratory testing is unavailable.
Speed at the anaerobic threshold is a key physiological predictor of half-marathon performance in recreational runners.
Peak treadmill speed during an incremental test is a strong predictor of half-marathon race performance.
